extra.reason: Rewrote MAP.md. Removed the false claim that the Day-convolution route 'was deleted as unused' — it came back as Foundations/{BoxMonoidal,DayTensor,BPTensor}.lean and is load-bearing for the braid work (BPTensor's own docstring: 'K ⊗ L is the Day convolution of the underlying presheaves'). Added the tensor to the reuse table with an explicit warning not to infer its absence from the deleted filenames. Stripped the [✓] status checklist, the [M0-M6] milestone tags, and the 'Build status' section (all → beads). Kept the 'do NOT re-derive' reuse table (the file's real value) and the Λ-junction/non-mono-fold gotcha in NonTriviality. Added the two undocumented files (Associativity.lean, FlagsComp.lean).

AGENTS.md 128 lines -> 11 + the two bd-managed blocks. It stated the bd quick reference three times and said nothing about Lean. Now a pointer to CLAUDE.md. Its one unique paragraph (non-interactive shell flags: aliases may add -i and hang the agent) migrated to CLAUDE.md's essentials.

Root DESIGN.md: fixed the stale functor names (Ch is NOTATION for the object type ChainCat.Obj K; the functor is chFunctor — Ch.obj/Ch.mapAut do not parse), added that as an explicit gotcha, switched □^∨(dims) to the live ⋁dims notation, added Flow/ to the folder list, deleted the 'Sorry inventory' section (4th copy of one fact; the rule lives in CLAUDE.md), and restated the file's purpose as a decisions log, not a status board. Schedule/DESIGN.md: repaired the broken §3 table (rows L10-L13 and F were orphaned AFTER the prose, rendering as a stray second table — folded back in), fixed the mis-numbered hypothesis budget (said L8 = thin, but L8 is IsOpen(star a); thinness is L13), and removed two references to Schedule/Chart.lean, a file that was never created (the material lives in HDA.lean / LabelChart.lean / Sculpture.lean). The open L9 row is now a bead. Kept L7's ❌ FALSE marker — a refutation is timeless, not status.

Rewrote /orient (140 -> 64 lines). Deleted: status/sorry-frontier section (bd owns status), module map (ARCHITECTURE.md owns it; the copy omitted Cobordisms/ and Flow/), all [[wikilink]]s into the private memory dir, the wrong settings.json allowlist claim, the '(was Operations/)' provenance note, and the MEMORY.md pointer (no such file in-repo). Preserved + re-verified: off-the-shelf mathlib API table (every module path checked against .lake/packages/mathlib), the '3-line def beats a 40-line bespoke structure / Chains/Slice.lean is the exemplar' rule, the gotchas (rigid Aut(box^n), erw-not-rw + the instance-mismatch reason, yonedaEquiv motive failure, K.toPsh dot notation, List.get_map / Fin.cast), build commands + trust-lake-build-not-the-IDE, never-read-.lake + use Explore. Ends by pointing at 'bd ready' and ARCHITECTURE.md. Closed with --force: dep Cubical-7oj (ARCHITECTURE.md refresh) is independent of this rewrite.

Rewrote ARCHITECTURE.md. Added: Flow/ (Flow.lean, ChainConcat.lean); the braid-enrichment headline (hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure, braidFunctor, the flow 2-category) with a pointer to BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md; and all ~30 previously undocumented files (Salvetti/ 24 files grouped into executions/functor/Salvetti-bridge, Schedule/ 16 files grouped into atlas/events/localCOM/label-layer, Chains/{ChainSlice,CubeNonSelfLinked,SegalProd,SegalSplit}, Arrangements/ all 12, Cobordisms/{Associativity,FlagsComp}). Every non-Testing .lean file in the tree is now named; every cited path verified with find/ls. Removed the 'Open questions — cylinder track' roadmap (filed as Cubical-82k, Cubical-ei2, Cubical-ngh, Cubical-cw1) and all status framing ('sorry-free', 'is proved'). Kept verbatim: the 'Read this first' line, the 'Where do I find...?' index (extended, not altered), and the Build & conventions block + notation (added one gotcha: equivWedgeCat/refineToWedge/RefineConcat.append carry NSL+altitude).

Repo files no longer link into the private memory store (fixed earlier: ARCHITECTURE.md x2, orient SKILL.md x7, and Chains/Segal.lean:817 — a LEAN SOURCE FILE that said 'See [[cubechains-segal]]', a dangling pointer for anyone who clones the repo).

MEMORY.md rewritten from a dated status board (✅/🚧/'NEXT =' paragraphs) into what it was always supposed to be: a one-line-per-memory index. Its first line used to order every agent to read CLEANUP.md, which does not exist.

Deleted 6 memories that were status, roadmap, or stale — 34 files -> 28, zero orphans:
- cubechains-deferred-sorries: WRONG. Claimed the repo is sorry-free 'except Research/Conjectures.lean'. CubeChains/Research/ does not exist, and there are zero sorries anywhere outside quarantine.
- cubechains-cleanup-pass: pure process history of a past refactor. git has it.
- cubechains-braid-final: about the quarantined FinalPrecubical/ folder, plus a 'NEXT =' roadmap.
- finalbraid-salcom: about the FinalBraid/ folder, deleted in the 2026-07-11 reorg; its own top note admitted every path was pre-reorg.
- cubechains-cylinder-roadmap: roadmap -> now beads Cubical-82k/ei2/ngh/cw1.
- cubechains-moore-monoid-main: status.

Kept: the 8 type:feedback memories (how to work with the user — the one thing that legitimately lives in the agent-memory system) and the durable math (refutations, notation, landmines).

NOTE for the future: bd remember is NOT git-portable (see the beads-memory-is-not-portable memory). Durable facts that must survive a clone belong in a PINNED BEAD or in git, not in the memory store.

MORSE.md §5's build-order table (6 rows, with a 'depends on' column — it was already a dependency graph) is now the e-morse epic (Cubical-dhc) with 6 beads and real edges: Grading + Diamond block CubeChainComplex; CubeChainComplex + TimeFunction block DiscreteMorse. Each bead carries the warning that applies to it — in particular morse-5 (DiscreteMorse) records that the local matchings DO NOT glue and a time function does not fix it (8-cycle on fourSquare), so nobody starts from the refuted §4(a). §5 in the file is now four lines pointing at the epic. The exposition (§1-§4, the forced (−1)^j sign, the w₁ story, the Betti table) and the two ⚠ refutation boxes stay as prose — they are timeless, not status.

Stripped process/provenance narration from .lean docstrings across the braid critical path (Foundations/, Chains/, Arrangements/, Salvetti/). 50 files changed, comments only: verified mechanically that the comment-stripped source is byte-identical to HEAD for all 68 scoped .lean files. Removed: all 'sorry-free'/'PROVED'/'green' status badges; all 'unconditional'/'assumption-free' self-justification (Slice.lean x7, ChainSkeletal.lean x9) — replaced where load-bearing by a plain fact about the signature ('for every K'); all 'Phase N'/'DELIVERABLE 1-6'/'Item N'/'[RESULT 1]'/'[IN PROGRESS]'/'now discharged' process markers; comparisons to deleted files (Operations/Cylinder, Ch'.concatMor) and one dangling ClaudeSetup.md pointer; '**Layer:**/**Imports:**' boilerplate; signature restatements; module docstrings cut toward the 10-line budget. KEPT: every ASCII commuting diagram, BlockDecomp.lean blockFace_spec square, Segal.lean three-equivalence fenced block, all genuine gotchas (erw-not-rw, defeq shortcuts, heartbeat notes, the two Correspondence counterexamples justifying NSL+altitude), literature refs. Comment lines: Foundations 1141->1045, Chains 1618->1428, Arrangements 444->414, Salvetti 1469->1397 (total 4672->4284, -388). BUILD GREEN: lake build CubeChains, 2010 jobs, zero errors (only pre-existing unusedSimpArgs linter warnings in Braiding.lean, untouched code).

DONE. lake build CubeChains green (2010 jobs), zero sorry outside quarantine.

MEASURED: 36,844 lines of Lean vs ~4,200 lines of prose ABOUT the project. 9,216 comment lines = 29.4% of non-blank Lean.

THE FOUR DOCS THAT WERE ACTIVELY WRONG (not merely stale):
1. README.md advertised a REFUTED theorem — 'So Ch(K) and V are homotopy equivalent!' — while LabelSpace.lean:237 says that step 'fails for branching K' and Testing/TwoSquares.lean is the machine-checked counterexample. (User has since rewritten the README themselves.)
2. CLAUDE.md contradicted itself 30 lines apart: :14 'MEMORY.md is the live status board, keep it updated' vs :44 'do NOT use MEMORY.md files'.
3. Schedule/DESIGN.md asserted lemma L7 at :289 having REFUTED it at :262.
4. Salvetti/BRAID.md's headline object G K := ⨆ ConcGrpd(K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ does not exist in the code (no ⋊, no K^⊗n, no BrMonCat anywhere). Superseded by BraidFunctor.lean's ConcCatN, which grades by event count in ONE K. SUPERSEDED banner added; the rebase-or-retire call is Cubical-w7x, left open because it is a MATH decision tied to the paper.
Plus: a memory auto-loaded every session claimed the repo was sorry-free 'except Research/Conjectures.lean' — that folder does not exist and there are zero sorries.

STRUCTURAL FIX: tracked files pointed into ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/, a machine-local dir that is NOT in git — including a LEAN SOURCE FILE (Chains/Segal.lean:817, 'See [[cubechains-segal]]'). Anyone cloning the repo got dangling pointers into one laptop. All removed.

DIAGNOSIS of why this happened (the real deliverable): the junk prose is DEFENSIVE, not explanatory — 'sorry-free' x10, 'unconditional' x7 in one file, 'assumption-free' x7 in another. All true, all worthless to a reader, all addressed to the requester. It is a status report to a grader, misfiled into a source file. Prior instruction attempts failed because they were PROHIBITIONS WITHOUT DESTINATIONS: 'don't write process prose' lost to 'leave the next agent enough context', which had a concrete affordance (a markdown file) while the prohibition had none. Beads supplies the missing destination.

THE RULE, now in CLAUDE.md: if a fact would become false when a bead closes, it belongs in the bead, not in a file. Plus three MECHANICAL tests (would it become false on close? does it have a tense? does it read as an argument that your work is good?), budgets not adjectives (<=1 line of intent per declaration, <=10 per module docstring), and a destinations table. Key correction discovered mid-work: bd remember is NOT git-portable (issues.jsonl exports only _type:issue; the Dolt DB is gitignored; a clone fetches refs/heads/* only) — so durable knowledge that must survive a clone goes in a PINNED BEAD or in git, and CLAUDE.md stays the constitution because it is the only thing that loads when bd is absent.

Remaining work is tracked under Cubical-ndf. Open: w7x (BRAID.md, needs the user's math call).

AUDIT DONE. Two headline findings.

1. UNCONDITIONALITY: CONFIRMED, by Lean rather than by reading imports.
   hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure : ∀ (K : BPSet), HasGlobalEventNaming K ↔ BraidPure K
   axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Same for braidFunctor.
   Landmine #1 (equivWedgeCat silently carrying NSL/AdmitsAltitude) CANNOT be present: a
   hypothesis-free statement that compiles sorry-free with no extra axioms has nowhere to hide a
   side condition. Importing a file that merely CONTAINS conditional theorems is harmless. The
   import cone is therefore irrelevant to unconditionality — it only matters for the file surgery.

2. THE §8.1 CLAIM IS WRONG. Cone = 58 of 124 files: Salvetti 18, Chains 12, SCHEDULE 11,
   Arrangements 10, Foundations 7. §8.1 says the braid result needs THREE Schedule files
   (EventNaming, EventMapBij, Orientation). It actually pulls in ELEVEN — including Space, Cover,
   Cone, ChainCone, Atlas, HDA, EventLocalSystem, and LabelSpace. The 'schedule space, atlas, cones,
   charts' that §8.1 tells you to quarantine are INSIDE the cone. Only 5 of 16 Schedule files
   (COMSheaf, Horizon, LabelChart, LocalCOM, Sculpture) are quarantinable as things stand.

BUT all 11 enter through exactly THREE ACCIDENTAL EDGES, each thin:
  a) Salvetti/BraidFunctor -> Schedule/Orientation, for TWO names: orSign, eventEquiv_id.
     This single import drags in Cover -> LabelSpace and the whole schedule-space stack.
     orSign/eventEquiv_id are EVENT-layer facts; they sit in Orientation.lean only because that is
     where w₁ happened to be developed.
  b) Salvetti/Normalize -> Schedule/Atlas, for SEVEN names: bslice, pbead, psz, psz_pos, pcubes,
     pchain, prefine. Verified: Atlas lines ~640-940 are pure CHAIN plumbing with zero references to
     Sched/Chart/Stratum/schedCone. They are misfiled — they belong in Chains/.
  c) Schedule/EventMapBij -> Schedule/Cone, for schedCone + 3 friends, used ONLY in the trailing
     corollaries (lines 375-386), AFTER eventMap_bijective (361) and eventMap_surjective (368). The
     bijectivity proof itself does not touch Cone.

ROOT CAUSE, and the thing §8.1 missed: eventEquiv is DEFINED IN Schedule/Space.lean:52 —
  eventEquiv f := Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f)
An event-layer definition parked inside the schedule-SPACE module. That is why Space (and hence the
atlas/cone/chart stack) is in the cone at all. §5 is right that eventMap_bijective is load-bearing;
it is just in the wrong folder.

THE FIX — an Events/ layer, which makes ALL of Schedule/ quarantinable (11 -> 0):
  Events/EventNaming.lean        (move; already clean — imports only Salvetti.Lines + Foundations.Altitude)
  Events/EventLocalSystem.lean   (move; already clean)
  Events/EventMapBij.lean        (move; leave the 4 cone corollaries behind in Schedule/)
  Events/EventEquiv.lean         (EXTRACT eventEquiv + eventObjLinearOrder + card_eventObj_eq_of_hom from Space.lean)
  Events/OrdSign.lean            (EXTRACT ordSign/ordPerm/ordSign_trans/orSign/eventEquiv_id from Orientation.lean)
  Chains/ChainPartition.lean     (EXTRACT the ~290-line p*/bslice block from Atlas.lean)
Then Salvetti/ imports only Events/, and the schedule space, atlas, cones, charts, LabelSpace,
Orientation's w₁, COMSheaf, Horizon, Sculpture, LabelChart, LocalCOM all quarantine cleanly.

ACTION: BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §8.1 needs correcting — it names the wrong three files and
underestimates the surgery. iso-events (Cubical-7sa) rewritten with this plan.

DONE. lake build CubeChains green (2012 jobs), zero sorry, axiom-clean.

RESULT: the braid cone went 58 -> 52 files, and Schedule/ went 11 -> ZERO.
  before: Salvetti 18, Chains 12, SCHEDULE 11, Arrangements 10, Foundations 7
  after:  Salvetti 18, Chains 13, Arrangements 10, Foundations 7, EVENTS 4, Schedule 0
Theorem unchanged: hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure : ∀ (K : BPSet), HasGlobalEventNaming K ↔ BraidPure K
Axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

NEW LAYER CubeChains/Events/ (imports only Chains/, Foundations/, Salvetti/{Lines,SalBraidPartition}):
  EventNaming.lean       (moved; + the Fintype (EventObj a) instance, which belongs next to EventObj)
  EventLocalSystem.lean  (moved; + dimSum_eq_of_hom and card_eventObj_eq_of_hom, pulled out of ChainCone
                          where they were misfiled — they need only eventObj_card + serialWedge_dimSum_eq)
  EventMapBij.lean       (moved; dropped the Schedule/Cone import; + eventEquiv, eventEquiv_id, eventEquiv_comp)
  OrdSign.lean           (new; ordPerm/ordSign + lemmas, eventObjLinearOrder, evFin, orSign, orSign_comp, Orientable)
Plus Chains/ChainPartition.lean (the bslice/pbead/psz/pcubes/pchain/prefine block lifted out of Schedule/Atlas —
pure chain plumbing, verified Space-free).

ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED AND FIXED: eventEquiv was defined in Schedule/Space.lean:52 as
Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f) — an event-layer definition parked inside the
schedule-SPACE module. That one misfiling is why Space/Atlas/Cone/LabelSpace were in the braid cone.

THREE THINGS THE PLAN DID NOT ANTICIPATE (all forced, all resolved):
1. Normalize also needed the Fintype (EventObj a) INSTANCE, which it was getting through Atlas's import
   chain — invisible to a name-based analysis. Moved to EventNaming next to EventObj.
2. eventEquiv_comp lived in Schedule/Cover.lean:86, and orSign_comp needs it. It went to Events/EventMapBij
   with eventEquiv (putting it in OrdSign would have forced Cover to import OrdSign).
3. Orientable + intUnits_inv_self are used by Salvetti/BraidCharacters (evPerm_sign_coboundary_of_orientable).
   So Orientable had to move to Events/OrdSign; leaving it behind would have kept the edge alive.
   Schedule/Orientation keeps exactly orChar + orientable_of_hasGlobalEventNaming + orientable_of_labelling
   — the w1 story — and its Schedule/Cover import turned out to be DEAD and was dropped.

REMAINING EDGE: Salvetti/SalLocal.lean -> Schedule/LocalCOM. SalLocal is OFF the braid cone (PurityHDA
never reaches it), so it quarantines WITH Schedule/ rather than blocking it. See the note on Cubical-217.
